info.title: Music Catalog API
info.description: A JSON:API music catalogue — albums, artists, tracks, playlists and libraries.

get.description: Returns a paginated collection of `albums` resources.

parameters.name: filter[title]
parameters.description: Matches values containing the given substring.

parameters.name: filter[rating]
parameters.description: Matches values within the given inclusive numeric range (min/max, either optional).

parameters.name: filter[releasedAt]
parameters.description: Matches values within the given inclusive date-time range (min/max ISO-8601, either optional).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of sort fields. Prefix a field with `-` for descending order. Allowed tokens: `title`, `-title`, `releasedAt`, `-releasedAt`, `status`, `-status`.

parameters.name: fields[albums]
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of `albums` fields to return (sparse fieldsets). Allowed members: `title`, `averageRating`, `artwork`, `releasedAt`, `explicit`, `status`, `availableFrom`, `availableUntil`, `releaseInfo`, `artist`, `tracks`.

parameters.name: page[size]
parameters.description: The number of resources per page.

parameters.name: withCount
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of relationship-count tokens (the Countable profile). `_self_` counts this collection; a relation name counts that relation per item. Recognised only when the Countable profile is negotiated. Allowed tokens: `tracks`.

400.description: Bad Request — the request was malformed (e.g. an invalid query parameter).

403.description: Forbidden — the request is not authorised.

406.description: Not Acceptable — the `Accept` header could not be satisfied.

401.description: Unauthorized — authentication is required and was missing or invalid.

post.summary: Apply a batch of atomic operations
post.description: Applies an ordered batch of write operations all-or-nothing within a single transaction (the JSON:API Atomic Operations extension). Negotiated via the `ext="https://jsonapi.org/ext/atomic"` media-type parameter on both the request `Content-Type` and the `Accept` header. Either every operation is committed or none is; a failure rolls the whole batch back. The batch commits through a single transactional persister.

requestBody.description: The batch of operations. Sent under the extension-qualified `application/vnd.api+json; ext="https://jsonapi.org/ext/atomic"` media type.

200.description: The result of each operation, in batch order.

400.description: Bad Request — the operations document was malformed.

403.description: Forbidden — the request is not authorised.

404.description: Not Found — an operation targets a resource that does not exist.

406.description: Not Acceptable — the `Accept` header did not request the atomic extension.

409.description: Conflict — an operation conflicts with the resource state (e.g. a type or id mismatch).

415.description: Unsupported Media Type — the `Content-Type` did not carry the atomic `ext` parameter.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity — an operation in the batch failed validation. The whole batch is rolled back.

401.description: Unauthorized — authentication is required and was missing or invalid.

AlbumStatus.description:
| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `upcoming` | Announced but not yet on sale. |
| `released` | Released and available to stream or buy. |
| `withdrawn` | Withdrawn from the catalogue (back-catalogue or rights lapsed). |
x-enum-descriptions: Announced but not yet on sale.
Released and available to stream or buy.
Withdrawn from the catalogue (back-catalogue or rights lapsed).

AlbumsAtomicAdd.description: The resource object an `add` operation creates.

lid.description: A local id assigned to the created resource, referenceable by later operations in the batch.

AlbumsAtomicUpdate.description: The resource object an `update` operation writes.

lid.description: The local id of a resource created earlier in the batch.

oneOf.title: Targeted by ref/href
oneOf.description: Neither `id` nor `lid` in `data` — the operation targets the resource by its `ref` or `href`.

releasedAt.description: Value must be before a dynamically-resolved date/time bound.

status.description: Where the album sits in its release lifecycle.

availableUntil.description: Value is compared against the `availableFrom` field (>).

releasedAt.description: Value must be before a dynamically-resolved date/time bound.

status.description: Where the album sits in its release lifecycle.

availableUntil.description: Value is compared against the `availableFrom` field (>).

releasedAt.description: Value must be before a dynamically-resolved date/time bound.

status.description: Where the album sits in its release lifecycle.

availableUntil.description: Value is compared against the `availableFrom` field (>).

AtomicOperation.description: One operation in the batch. The target is given by exactly one of `ref` or `href`.

ref.description: A structural reference to the operation's target (an alternative to `href`). Identifies a resource by `type` plus exactly one of `id` or `lid`; an optional `relationship` narrows the target to that relationship.

lid.description: A local id referencing a resource created earlier in the same batch.

href.description: A URI reference to the operation's target (an alternative to `ref`).

data.description: The operation payload. Its shape depends on `op` and the target: a resource object for an `add`/`update`; a resource identifier (or `null`) for a to-one relationship; an array of resource identifiers for a to-many relationship; absent for a resource `remove`.

AtomicOperationsRequest.description: The Atomic Operations extension request document: an ordered, all-or-nothing batch of write operations.

atomic:operations.description: The ordered list of operations to apply. Applied in array order, all-or-nothing within one transaction.

AtomicResult.description: The result fragment of one operation. Carries `data` and/or `meta` only; an empty object `{}` is valid (e.g. a `remove`).

data.description: The created or updated resource (or its identifier) the operation returns.

AtomicResultsResponse.description: The Atomic Operations extension response document: one result fragment per applied operation, in batch order.

atomic:results.description: The result of each operation, in batch order.

JsonApi.description: An object describing the server's implementation of the JSON:API specification.

LinkObject.description: A JSON:API link: a string URI, a link object, or null (e.g. an absent pagination link).

Meta.description: A JSON:API meta object: a free-form set of non-standard members.

UsersAtomicAdd.description: The resource object an `add` operation creates.

lid.description: A local id assigned to the created resource, referenceable by later operations in the batch.

UsersAtomicUpdate.description: The resource object an `update` operation writes.

lid.description: The local id of a resource created earlier in the batch.

oneOf.title: Targeted by ref/href
oneOf.description: Neither `id` nor `lid` in `data` — the operation targets the resource by its `ref` or `href`.
